title: cytisus supinus subsp. austriacus
intro: Cytisus supinus subsp. austriacus, also known as Alpine broom, is a subspecies of shrub belonging to the genus Cytisus, characterized by its small yellow flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cytisus supinus subsp. austriacus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a variety of uses in natural medicine.
